findings.originalTitle: Amoxicillin-EBV rash described as 'pathognomonic' — overstated specificity
findings.evidence: "the amoxicillin-associated maculopapular rash is essentially pathognomonic for EBV mono"
findings.suggestion: Replace 'pathognomonic' with 'highly characteristic' or 'strongly suggestive'; the rash also occurs in CMV mononucleosis and other viral syndromes.
findings.triageReason: Wave 3b added EBV AMOX RASH MECHANISM RULE which explicitly forbids 'pathognomonic' framing and requires 'highly characteristic' language.

findings.originalTitle: EBV-amoxicillin rash mechanism stated as 'immune complex-mediated' — disputed/inaccurate
findings.evidence: "the mechanism is immune complex-mediated rather than IgE-mediated"
findings.suggestion: State mechanism as T-cell mediated / p-i concept, not immune complex (Type III).
findings.triageReason: Wave 3b EBV AMOX RASH MECHANISM RULE requires T-cell mediated language; immune-complex description forbidden.

findings.originalTitle: Monospot sensitivity range understated for first week of illness
findings.evidence: "has variable sensitivity early in illness (as low as 70-80% in the first week)"
findings.suggestion: Correct to 25–50% sensitivity in week 1, rising to ~85% by week 3.
findings.triageReason: Wave 3b MONOSPOT SENSITIVITY RULE mandates 25–50% week-1 sensitivity; 70–80% explicitly forbidden.

findings.originalTitle: SCD case: MCV 79 fL labeled low — inconsistent with classic HbSS MCV
findings.evidence: "MCV": "79", "unit": "fL", "referenceRange": "80-100", "status": "low"
findings.suggestion: Set MCV to normocytic range (86–92 fL) for uncomplicated HbSS, or add co-diagnosis explanation.
findings.triageReason: Wave 3b HbSS MCV RULE forbids MCV < 80 fL in pure HbSS cases without co-diagnosis explanation.

findings.originalTitle: HbF 6% flagged 'high' but is expected/therapeutic in untreated HbSS
findings.evidence: "HbF": "6", "unit": "%", "referenceRange": "< 2", "status": "high"
findings.suggestion: Add case-specific note clarifying elevated HbF is beneficial in HbSS; reference range context is for normal adults only.
findings.triageReason: No specific rule covers HbF reference-range labeling in HbSS. The HbSS MCV rule addresses MCV only. This case-level data error needs a new B_case_gen_rules entry.

findings.originalTitle: Dark urine morning predominance attributed to SCD — suggests PNH pattern, not SCD
findings.evidence: "the dark urine does seem worse in the mornings when I first wake up"
findings.suggestion: Remove morning-predominant pattern from SCD hidden history; reserve this for PNH.
findings.triageReason: Wave 3b MORNING-DARK-URINE SPECIFICITY RULE forbids morning-predominant dark urine in SCD cases; reserves it for PNH.

findings.originalTitle: HPI states 'no prior similar episodes' — contradicted by hidden history dactylitis
findings.evidence: HPI: "He has no prior similar episodes" vs. hiddenHistory: "He confirms experiencing hand and foot swelling as an infant"
findings.suggestion: Revise HPI to 'no prior formally evaluated episodes' or 'no prior hospitalizations for pain crises'.
findings.triageReason: Wave 3b HPI-HIDDEN HISTORY NON-CONTRADICTION RULE forbids outright denial in HPI of symptoms present in hiddenHistory.

findings.originalTitle: Peripheral Blood Smear listed under 'availableImaging' — incorrect categorization
findings.evidence: "availableImaging": ["Peripheral Blood Smear", "Abdominal Ultrasound", "Chest X-Ray"]
findings.suggestion: Move PBS to availableLabs.
findings.triageReason: Wave 3b rules address test-result gating but no CRITICAL_RULE explicitly enforces PBS/ECG/Thoracentesis categorization into correct arrays. Needs new B rule for availableImaging exclusion list.

findings.originalTitle: Student's submitted diagnosis is Hereditary Spherocytosis — graded incorrect, but score 72 awarded despite correct workup
findings.evidence: "diagnosis": "Hereditary Spherocytosis", "correct": false, "score": 72
findings.suggestion: Apply harder penalty floor for wrong diagnosis at Foundations level.
findings.triageReason: Wave 2 added WRONG DIAGNOSIS cap in rubric.mjs (diagnosisCompleteness ≤ dcLowMax when correct=false). Wave 10 confirmed closed.

findings.originalTitle: Student adds 'likely concurrent medial meniscus injury' — MRI shows no meniscal tear, yet diagnosisAccuracy penalized only partially
findings.evidence: Student diagnosis adds unconfirmed meniscal co-diagnosis contradicted by MRI; diagnosisAccuracy score 30.
findings.suggestion: Add rubric guidance that unconfirmed co-diagnoses contradicted by imaging incur defined deduction.
findings.triageReason: Wave 3c added CONTRADICTED CO-DIAGNOSIS RULE: deduct ≥6 pts from diagnosisAccuracy when co-diagnosis contradicted by available evidence.

findings.originalTitle: Teaching point states Lachman test sensitivity ~85% — accepted but context missing
findings.evidence: "The Lachman test ... is the most sensitive physical exam maneuver for ACL tear (~85% sensitivity)"
findings.suggestion: Add qualifier 'in experienced hands' or note sensitivity drops in acute swollen knee.
findings.triageReason: No existing rule covers Lachman sensitivity qualifier requirement. A B_case_gen_rules entry should require examiner-experience caveat for quoted sensitivity figures in MSK teaching points.

findings.originalTitle: No physical examination data provided — limits ACL diagnostic reasoning
findings.evidence: Student notes physical exam findings not shown for ACL case.
findings.suggestion: Include structured physical exam results panel (Lachman, anterior drawer, pivot shift, McMurray's).
findings.triageReason: Wave 3b MSK PHYSICAL EXAM PRESENCE RULE requires ACL cases to include physical exam findings in the case data.

findings.originalTitle: Case labels this a 'Hemolytic Crisis' but presentation lacks defining crisis features
findings.evidence: "correctDiagnosis": "Sickle Cell Disease (Hemolytic Crisis)" vs gradual onset, near-normal vitals.
findings.suggestion: Relabel to 'SCD with chronic hemolysis' or add explicit HPI language supporting acute crisis.
findings.triageReason: No existing rule requires correctDiagnosis label to match clinical acuity descriptors in HPI/vitals. Needs new B rule for SCD crisis framing consistency.

findings.originalTitle: Grading feedback mentions 'rash timeline' as missed but student reasoning addresses it
findings.evidence: missedQuestions cites rash timing as missed; student reasoning explicitly articulates the concept.
findings.suggestion: Differentiate 'did not ask' from 'did not demonstrate understanding' in grading feedback.
findings.triageReason: Wave 6 PROACTIVE ELICITATION RULE and KEY-QUESTION ELICITATION RULE: incidentally surfaced items must not appear in missedQuestions panel.

findings.originalTitle: Rumack-Matthew nomogram threshold values are incorrect
findings.evidence: "treatment line threshold is ~150 mcg/mL at 4h, ~75 mcg/mL at 8h"
findings.suggestion: Confirm correct treatment-line values (4h:150, 8h:75, 12h:37.5) and cite validated source.
findings.triageReason: Wave 3b RUMACK-MATTHEW THRESHOLD RULE added explicit time-point values (4h:150, 8h:75, 12h:37.5 mcg/mL).

findings.originalTitle: Urine drug screen lists acetaminophen as a screened analyte — standard UDS panels do not include acetaminophen
findings.evidence: "Acetaminophen (urine screen)": "value": "Positive"
findings.suggestion: Remove acetaminophen from UDS; serum quantitative level is the correct test.
findings.triageReason: Wave 2 added URINE DRUG SCREEN ANALYTE RULE explicitly banning acetaminophen from UDS panels.

findings.originalTitle: LFT elevations at 6 hours post-ingestion are inconsistent with known APAP toxicity timeline
findings.evidence: AST 58 / ALT 72 elevated at 6h yet teaching point says LFT elevations appear at Phase II (24–72h).
findings.suggestion: Set LFTs normal at 6h, or add explanation that large ingestions can cause early mild transaminase rise.
findings.triageReason: Wave 3b APAP LFT TIMING RULE explicitly requires LFTs to be normal in Phase I (0–24h) or adds explanatory note for large-ingestion exception.

findings.originalTitle: HPI states patient denies diaphoresis/pallor but hidden history reveals these symptoms were observed
findings.evidence: HPI denies diaphoresis; hiddenSymptoms: diaphoresis and pallor noted by nursing staff.
findings.suggestion: Move objective nursing findings to visible vitals/exam section; hidden objective findings are a false learning model.
findings.triageReason: Wave 3b HPI-HIDDEN HISTORY NON-CONTRADICTION RULE forbids outright denial when hidden history contradicts it. SAFETY-CRITICAL ELICITATION RULE addresses gating of objective findings.

findings.originalTitle: Student interview elicits maximal tenderness at anatomical snuffbox, inconsistent with confirmed Colles' fracture diagnosis
findings.evidence: Patient response points to snuffbox; Colles' fracture tenderness should be over dorsal distal radius.
findings.suggestion: Correct patient dialogue to dorsal wrist/distal radius tenderness.
findings.triageReason: Wave 3b COLLES vs SCAPHOID LOCALISATION RULE requires correct anatomical tenderness localization for each diagnosis.

findings.originalTitle: Grading credits student for hand X-ray ordering but student explicitly did NOT order it
findings.evidence: testsOrdered has wrist X-ray only; grading feedback says 'PA and lateral wrist X-rays plus hand X-rays' ordered.
findings.suggestion: Grading must enumerate only ordered tests in feedback.
findings.triageReason: Wave 5 GRADING TEXT FIDELITY RULE and ORDERED-TESTS SCORING CONTRACT forbid crediting/mentioning unordered tests.

findings.originalTitle: HPI states patient denies numbness/tingling, but hidden history reveals paresthesias are present
findings.evidence: HPI: denies numbness; hiddenHistory: mild paresthesias in thumb/index finger.
findings.suggestion: HPI should say 'has not volunteered' not 'denies'; paresthesias are present on direct questioning.
findings.triageReason: Wave 3b HPI-HIDDEN HISTORY NON-CONTRADICTION RULE forbids denial language when hidden history contradicts.

findings.originalTitle: Passive suicidal ideation present in hidden history but graded as simply 'not asked' — safety-critical nuance lost
findings.evidence: hiddenHistory: passive SI 'life feels pointless'; missedQuestions treats it as a simple missed question.
findings.suggestion: Grading feedback must explicitly state direct questioning would reveal passive SI, with management implications.
findings.triageReason: Wave 3b SAFETY-CRITICAL ELICITATION RULE requires passive SI to be flagged as safety-critical with management context in grading feedback.

findings.originalTitle: NAC treatment decision taught as 'pending nomogram' but nomogram-qualifying level already returned — missed active teaching moment
findings.evidence: APAP level 210 mcg/mL at 6h already above treatment line; student leaves NAC 'pending'.
findings.suggestion: Grading must note NAC should be started immediately given level clearly above treatment line.
findings.triageReason: Wave 8 APAP NAC TIMING RULE: if NAC not mentioned, cap clinicalReasoning ≤67% of max and name gap explicitly.

findings.originalTitle: Teaching point cites DSM-5 '6 criteria' but lists only 6 with worthlessness absent from symptom count
findings.evidence: Teaching point says 6 criteria but worthlessness is present in both HPI and hiddenHistory making 7.
findings.suggestion: State patient meets 7 of 9 DSM-5 criteria.
findings.triageReason: Wave 3b DSM CRITERION TEXT CONSISTENCY RULE requires enumerated criteria count to match the actual symptoms documented in the case.

findings.originalTitle: All three cases share the same patient name 'Dmitri Voloshyn' across unrelated presentations
findings.evidence: case-10, case-11, case-12 all use 'Dmitri Voloshyn'.
findings.suggestion: Assign distinct names per case.
findings.triageReason: Wave 3b/4 PATIENT NAME UNIQUENESS RULE + usedNames dedup mechanism prevent name reuse across cases in a batch.

findings.originalTitle: Bacterial meningitis case temp 99.8°F conflicts with classic triad teaching
findings.evidence: temp 99.8°F with pneumococcal meningitis / 4200 CSF WBC — implausible.
findings.suggestion: Raise temp to 102–104°F or document antipyretic use explaining low-grade reading.
findings.triageReason: No existing rule covers minimum temperature realism for bacterial meningitis cases. Needs new B_case_gen_rules entry.

findings.originalTitle: CSF RBC labelled 'normal' at 8 cells/µL in SAH vs meningitis differential
findings.evidence: CSF RBC 8 cells/µL, referenceRange 0–5, status 'normal' — should be abnormal.
findings.suggestion: Flag as abnormal/high to accurately reflect reference range and preserve SAH differential teaching.
findings.triageReason: No existing rule covers CSF RBC labeling consistency. Lab status flags must match reference range arithmetic. Needs new B_case_gen_rules entry.

findings.originalTitle: Petechial rash described as 'atypical for pneumococcal' — this framing is misleading
findings.evidence: Petechial rash labeled 'atypical for pneumococcal but present' without clarifying it is classic for meningococcal.
findings.suggestion: Either remove rash or explicitly state petechial rash should raise N. meningitidis suspicion.
findings.triageReason: No existing rule covers meningitis rash differential teaching. Needs new B_case_gen_rules entry for petechial rash attribution.

findings.originalTitle: Teaching point on LP delay threshold omits immunocompromise nuance incompletely
findings.evidence: CT-first indications listed omit new-onset seizure and age ≥60 per IDSA guidelines.
findings.suggestion: Add 'new-onset seizure, or age ≥60' to CT-first indication list.
findings.triageReason: No existing rule covers completeness of LP delay indications per IDSA guidelines. Needs new B_case_gen_rules entry.

findings.originalTitle: Student submitted SAH diagnosis but grading marks test ordering near-complete despite missing MRI
findings.evidence: MRI Brain with contrast not ordered; testOrdering score 17 with 'complete and appropriate workup' feedback.
findings.suggestion: MRI is pivotal test for bacterial meningitis; absence must trigger mandatory deduction and explicit feedback.
findings.triageReason: The ORDERED-TESTS SCORING CONTRACT addresses grading accuracy but no rule creates a 'pivotal test' mandatory deduction. cc-003 identifies this cross-cutting pattern. Needs C_grader_rules entry for pivotal-test floor.

findings.originalTitle: History score 14/20 awarded despite student missing 3 of 4 listed key questions
findings.evidence: 3 of 4 key questions missed; historyInterview score 14/20 (70%).
findings.suggestion: Recalibrate so missing 3 of 4 key questions yields ≤10/20.
findings.triageReason: SCORE↔FEEDBACK CONSISTENCY RULE addresses praise/score mismatch but does not impose a proportional floor per missed key question. cc-001 identifies this as a cross-cutting grader calibration gap. Needs C_grader_rules entry.

findings.originalTitle: Thoracentesis listed under availableImaging but not credited as a separate orderable test
findings.evidence: Thoracentesis in availableImaging; absence treated as 'minor gap' despite management-altering pleural results.
findings.suggestion: Upgrade missing thoracentesis to significant gap with commensurate deduction.
findings.triageReason: Thoracentesis miscategorized (cc-009 also flags this) AND grading underweights its absence. Needs two fixes: B_case_gen_rules for categorization and C_grader_rules for pivotal-test deduction.

findings.originalTitle: Pleural fluid analysis results shown despite thoracentesis not being ordered by student
findings.evidence: Thoracentesis not in testsOrdered; full pleural fluid results appear in labResults and student reasons from them.
findings.suggestion: Gate pleural fluid results behind thoracentesis order.
findings.triageReason: Wave 5 ORDERED-TESTS SCORING CONTRACT addresses grader-side scoring but the platform-level result-display gating (app/trainer/page.tsx:3389) must properly gate procedural results. cc-008 cross-cutting. Needs A_app_ui fix to verify server-side filter blocks unordered procedural results.

findings.originalTitle: Light's criteria LDH threshold stated incorrectly in teaching point
findings.evidence: Teaching point omits 'serum' qualifier and implies criteria must all be met simultaneously.
findings.suggestion: Rewrite: 'exudate if ANY ONE of: pleural/serum protein >0.5, pleural/serum LDH >0.6, or pleural LDH >2/3 upper limit of normal serum LDH.'
findings.triageReason: No existing rule covers Light's criteria language accuracy. Needs new B_case_gen_rules entry for empyema/pleural effusion cases.

findings.originalTitle: History score 15/20 awarded despite missing 2 of 4 key questions per grader's own feedback
findings.evidence: 2 of 4 key questions missed; historyInterview score 15/20 (75%).
findings.suggestion: Reduce to ~10–11/20 or document bonus-credit logic explicitly.
findings.triageReason: Same cross-cutting pattern as f-035 and cc-001. KEY-QUESTION ELICITATION RULE exists but no proportional floor is enforced. Needs C_grader_rules fix.

findings.originalTitle: Lipid panel results available at Hour 0 of NSTEMI — clinically unrealistic timing
findings.evidence: Lipid panel returned as actionable result acutely; acute MI invalidates LDL values.
findings.suggestion: Block lipid panel from acute NSTEMI order set or return note about acute-phase unreliability.
findings.triageReason: No existing rule covers lipid panel timing validity in acute MI. Needs new B_case_gen_rules entry.

findings.originalTitle: Student anchored on SAH despite non-thunderclap onset — no remediation pathway offered
findings.evidence: Grading identifies anchoring but offers no structured remediation walk-through.
findings.suggestion: Add targeted teaching intervention walking through the LP→meningitis decision pivot.
findings.triageReason: No existing rule requires grader to provide structured remediation for anchoring bias. Needs C_grader_rules entry for anchoring feedback template.

findings.originalTitle: D-Dimer elevated at 1.2 µg/mL FEU without explicit CT-PA correlation in feedback
findings.evidence: D-Dimer elevated in pneumonia/sepsis context; no teaching note on low specificity.
findings.suggestion: Add teaching note that D-dimer has low specificity in active infection/inflammation.
findings.triageReason: No existing rule requires D-dimer specificity teaching note in infected patient context. Needs new B_case_gen_rules entry.

findings.originalTitle: Teaching point states 'normal WBC' but CBC shows WBC 11.8 (elevated)
findings.evidence: Teaching point: 'a normal WBC does not exclude significant pancreatic injury' vs WBC 11.8 (status: high).
findings.suggestion: Correct to 'only mild leukocytosis' rather than 'normal WBC'.
findings.triageReason: GRADING TEXT FIDELITY RULE covers grader-side text; no rule enforces that teaching point prose must not contradict actual lab values. Needs new B_case_gen_rules entry for lab-value/teachingPoint consistency check.

findings.originalTitle: Ranson criteria teaching point cites 'age >55' but patient is 52
findings.evidence: Teaching point cites age >55 as risk factor; patient.age is 52.
findings.suggestion: Remove age >55 as applicable criterion for this patient.
findings.triageReason: No existing rule requires teaching point severity criteria to apply to the actual patient age in the case. Needs new B_case_gen_rules entry for patient-anchored teaching points.

findings.originalTitle: Triglycerides 310 mg/dL presented as incidental finding but meets threshold for hypertriglyceridemic pancreatitis
findings.evidence: TG 310 elevated; no teaching point distinguishing it from the 500–1000 mg/dL causative threshold.
findings.suggestion: Add teaching point explaining 310 is below the ~500–1000 mg/dL HTG-pancreatitis threshold.
findings.triageReason: No existing rule requires teaching points to resolve ambiguous secondary-cause lab values. cc-011 identifies this cross-cutting pattern. Needs B_case_gen_rules entry for unexplained abnormal lab teaching-point coverage.

findings.originalTitle: Grading penalizes student for citing vitals but vitals are explicitly provided in caseData
findings.evidence: Grading feedback says student cited unavailable vitals; vitals ARE in caseData (HR 108, temp 100.4, RR 20).
findings.suggestion: Remove fabrication language; acknowledge vitals are in case record.
findings.triageReason: cc-002 cross-cutting pattern. GRADING TEXT FIDELITY RULE exists but does not explicitly instruct grader to cross-reference student-cited values against caseData.vitals before issuing fabrication warnings. Needs C_grader_rules entry.

findings.originalTitle: Grading penalizes student for citing vitals not in case data, but vitals ARE in caseData
findings.evidence: Grading introduces 'minor fabrication concern' for vitals (HR 56, temp 97.1, BP 138/88) present in caseData.
findings.suggestion: Remove fabrication concern; vitals are legitimately documented.
findings.triageReason: Same cc-002 pattern as f-047. Needs C_grader_rules entry cross-referencing vitals before fabrication warning.

findings.originalTitle: Teaching point states full replacement levothyroxine dose for 'young healthy adults' but case patient is 52
findings.evidence: Teaching point presents both full-dose and low-dose options without anchoring to this specific patient.
findings.suggestion: Anchor recommendation to this 52-year-old patient with bradycardia: low-dose initiation is appropriate.
findings.triageReason: cc-013 cross-cutting pattern. No rule requires teaching points to anchor management to patient-specific factors. Needs B_case_gen_rules entry.

findings.originalTitle: TSH >10 threshold to distinguish hypothyroidism from depression cited incorrectly as definitive
findings.evidence: Teaching point contrasts elevated TSH vs euthyroid sick syndrome inaccurately; ESS has low/low-normal TSH.
findings.suggestion: Revise to clarify ESS presents with low TSH pattern, not elevated TSH.
findings.triageReason: Wave 10 addressed hypothyroidism anemia rule but this TSH/ESS contrast error is distinct. Needs new B_case_gen_rules entry.

findings.originalTitle: Renal ultrasound report states kidneys are 'enlarged' but finding labeled as consistent with 'chronic' nephropathy
findings.evidence: Enlarged kidneys noted; no explanation that early DKD causes hyperfiltration-driven enlargement.
findings.suggestion: Add brief note explaining early DKD enlargement vs small kidneys in other CKD causes.
findings.triageReason: No rule covers kidney size teaching in DKD. Needs new B_case_gen_rules entry for DKD renal ultrasound teaching context.

findings.originalTitle: Patient already on lisinopril but teaching point presents ACE inhibitor as a new management recommendation
findings.evidence: hiddenHistory.medications includes lisinopril; teaching point says 'initiate ACE inhibitor'.
findings.suggestion: Frame as 'optimize existing ACE inhibitor therapy' not initiation.
findings.triageReason: No rule requires teaching point management recommendations to account for medications already in hiddenHistory. Needs new B_case_gen_rules entry.

findings.originalTitle: No serology for secondary causes of nephrotic syndrome available despite teaching point raising this concern
findings.evidence: Teaching point says exclude secondary causes; ANA/complement/SPEP not in availableLabs.
findings.suggestion: Add ANA, complement, SPEP, hepatitis serologies, anti-PLA2R to availableLabs.
findings.triageReason: No rule requires teaching-point-referenced workup to be orderable in the case. Wave 3b KEY-QUESTION HIDDEN-ANSWER COVERAGE addresses questions but not lab availability. Needs B_case_gen_rules entry.

findings.originalTitle: CT Severity Index 3 (Grade C) reported but CTSI scoring methodology not explained in teaching points
findings.evidence: CTSI score appears in imaging result; teaching points reference Ranson/BISAP but not CTSI.
findings.suggestion: Add teaching point explaining CTSI (Balthazar A–E + necrosis, 0–10 scale), interpreting score 3 as mild.
findings.triageReason: Wave 3b BALTHAZAR vs MCTSI rule added scoring system distinction but does not require CTSI methodology explanation when score appears in imaging results. Needs B_case_gen_rules entry.

findings.originalTitle: Macrocytic anemia present but B12/folate not available to order, limiting differential workup
findings.evidence: MCV 102 elevated; B12/folate absent from availableLabs.
findings.suggestion: Add B12 and folate to availableLabs.
findings.triageReason: Same pattern as f-053: teaching points reference a differential that students cannot actively pursue. Needs B_case_gen_rules entry for macrocytosis workup availability.

findings.originalTitle: Normal uric acid mislabeled as supporting gouty etiology in reasoning feedback
findings.evidence: Student writes 'Elevated uric acid supports gouty etiology'; uric acid is 6.1 (normal). Grading does not flag error in top-level feedback.
findings.suggestion: Top-level feedback must flag the student's factual error in misreading a normal result as elevated.
findings.triageReason: GRADING TEXT FIDELITY RULE prevents inventing data but doesn't require flagging when student misreads lab values. cc-010 adjacent. Needs C_grader_rules entry for student-misread-value correction.

findings.originalTitle: Student states 'elevated uric acid' but result is normal; grading does not flag this as an error
findings.evidence: Uric acid 6.1 (normal); student writes 'elevated'; overall feedback 'Excellent diagnostic performance' with no correction.
findings.suggestion: Grader must evaluate student interpretive statements against actual lab values and flag misreadings.
findings.triageReason: Same as f-057 — grader needs an explicit rule to flag when student's written interpretation of a lab value contradicts the actual result. Needs C_grader_rules entry.

findings.originalTitle: Rai staging explanation conflates AIHA-driven anemia with Rai III criteria without caveat
findings.evidence: Teaching point: 'AIHA places him at Rai stage III'; iwCLL 2018 distinguishes immune cytopenias from disease-related cytopenias for staging.
findings.suggestion: Clarify AIHA is an independent treatment indication; Rai staging uses marrow-failure cytopenias, not immune cytopenias.
findings.triageReason: No existing rule covers CLL staging accuracy. Needs new B_case_gen_rules entry for hematologic staging nuance.

findings.originalTitle: MCV of 101 fL unexplained and potentially misleading in CLL/AIHA context
findings.evidence: MCV 101 fL (high), no explanation anywhere in case.
findings.suggestion: Add note: reticulocytosis from AIHA pushes MCV up; this is physiologically consistent.
findings.triageReason: cc-011 cross-cutting: unexplained abnormal lab values must have teaching point. No existing rule covers this for CLL/AIHA context. Needs B_case_gen_rules entry.

findings.originalTitle: Flow cytometry reference range for CD19 is clinically inaccurate and internally inconsistent
findings.evidence: CD19 referenceRange: 'Negative on mature lymphocytes in abnormal populations' — CD19 is normally positive on B cells.
findings.suggestion: Correct reference range: CD19 positive on normal B cells; abnormality is CD5 co-expression.
findings.triageReason: No rule covers flow cytometry reference range accuracy. Needs B_case_gen_rules entry for CLL immunophenotyping reference ranges.

findings.originalTitle: PSA teaching point contradicted by grading: ordered with 'appropriate self-awareness' despite no diagnostic value
findings.evidence: AUA advises against PSA during acute prostatitis; grading praises ordering it as 'sound test-utilization reasoning'.
findings.suggestion: Flag PSA ordering in acute prostatitis as test-utilization concern; add teaching note about post-treatment recheck.
findings.triageReason: Wave 3b RESOURCE STEWARDSHIP rule addresses general stewardship but no specific PSA-in-prostatitis prohibition exists. Needs B_case_gen_rules + C_grader_rules entries.

findings.originalTitle: historyInterview score of 15 internally inconsistent with two missed key questions
findings.evidence: 2 of 4 key questions missed; historyInterview score 15.
findings.suggestion: Clarify scoring rubric or reduce score proportionally.
findings.triageReason: Same cc-001 / f-035 / f-039 pattern. KEY-QUESTION ELICITATION RULE exists but no proportional floor enforced. Needs C_grader_rules fix.

findings.originalTitle: DRE findings described in hidden history but never surfaced or graded
findings.evidence: DRE findings in hiddenHistory; never presented to student; not graded despite being diagnostic.
findings.suggestion: Add DRE as a physical examination interaction step with grading weight.
findings.triageReason: cc-010 cross-cutting: high-value hidden findings with no reveal path provide zero educational value. Needs B_case_gen_rules entry requiring DRE to be gated on physical-exam key question for GU cases.

findings.originalTitle: Hidden tophus finding never discoverable by student; clinically significant for staging
findings.evidence: Tophus in hiddenSymptoms; never revealed, never graded; changes disease classification.
findings.suggestion: Add tophus inquiry as key question with grading weight.
findings.triageReason: cc-010 cross-cutting. No rule requires that classification-changing findings be assigned a key-question trigger. Needs B_case_gen_rules entry.

findings.originalTitle: Working diagnosis 'Lymphoma' is inconsistent with student's submitted diagnosis of CLL
findings.evidence: workingDiagnosis: 'Lymphoma'; submitted: 'CLL with AIHA'; no feedback showing the diagnostic evolution.
findings.suggestion: Show working diagnosis evolution in feedback report.
findings.triageReason: Displaying workingDiagnosis evolution in the feedback report requires a schema/UI change to the scorecard output format. Out of current scope; deferred to schema-level Wave 12.

findings.originalTitle: Teaching point implies allopurinol is only urate-lowering therapy; febuxostat omitted
findings.evidence: Teaching point says 'e.g., allopurinol' without mentioning febuxostat.
findings.suggestion: Revise to 'e.g., allopurinol or febuxostat' for completeness.
findings.triageReason: No existing rule requires ULT teaching points to name alternative xanthine oxidase inhibitors. Needs B_case_gen_rules entry.

findings.originalTitle: Weekly goal counter shows 58 completed against a goal of 5
findings.evidence: 58 completions shown for weekly goal of 5.
findings.suggestion: Verify data source and scope to current ISO week.
findings.triageReason: Wave 2 confirmed false positive — WeeklyGoal correctly filters by completed_at >= weekStart. Count of 58 reflects legitimate dev/test sessions.

findings.originalTitle: Focus Areas card labels worst performers but top score drives recommendation
findings.evidence: Card header 'Focus areas' with all green scores (83–87%); recommendation picks 83%.
findings.suggestion: Rename card or surface genuinely low-performing areas.
findings.triageReason: Wave 3a WeakestSystems.tsx: header now shows 'Recent systems' when all top3 ≥75%. Confirmed in code at line 22.

findings.originalTitle: Recent activity scores shown as green/passing despite large sub-score losses
findings.evidence: 90% overall green but −31% Diagnosis Completeness sub-score hidden.
findings.suggestion: Display sub-score losses in warning color regardless of total score.
findings.triageReason: Wave 3a RecentActivity.tsx: loss chip links to /review#dim-<key> with tooltip. Sub-score loss is now visually surfaced via the chip.

findings.originalTitle: Onboarding modal blocks dashboard on every load for an existing user
findings.evidence: Welcome modal visible for user with 8-day streak and 58 completed cases.
findings.suggestion: Persist 'onboarding_completed' flag and suppress modal after first dismissal.
findings.triageReason: Wave 3 confirmed false positive — modal fires only when onboarding_complete flag is missing. Audit account legitimately lacks flag.

findings.originalTitle: Recommended next case rationale is too thin to drive deliberate practice
findings.evidence: 'Your Hematologic / Oncologic avg is 83.' — no dimension-level context.
findings.suggestion: Augment reason with specific weak dimension (e.g., 'Diagnosis Completeness 61% in Hem/Onc').
findings.triageReason: No prior wave addressed dimension-level context in the NextCaseCard recommendation rationale. Wave 3a added difficulty progression legend but not sub-dimension reasoning.

findings.originalTitle: Streak counter lacks context about what constitutes a 'streak day'
findings.evidence: '🔥 8 days streak' with no definition of what counts.
findings.suggestion: Add tooltip '≥1 case/day' to clarify streak definition.
findings.triageReason: No prior wave addressed streak-definition tooltip. Minor UX improvement, still actionable.

findings.originalTitle: Recent activity limited to same-day (May 16) entries only — no historical breadth
findings.evidence: All five recent-activity rows show May 16 only.
findings.suggestion: Display entries spanning at least the past 7 days.
findings.triageReason: This is a snapshot artifact of the audit account's single-day usage; the RecentActivity component is not capped to same-day. No code change needed.

findings.originalTitle: Streak label has spurious HTML comment nodes injected into the text
findings.suggestion: Rewrite JSX to use single template literal.
findings.triageReason: Wave 3/10 confirmed false positive — React comment nodes between adjacent JSX expressions are normal React behavior; screen readers ignore them. Already correct.

findings.originalTitle: React SSR hydration mismatch on Focus page causes silent DOM desync
findings.evidence: Inline color hex strings and font shorthands differ between SSR and client.
findings.suggestion: Use CSS custom properties or className-based theming instead of inline JS style objects.
findings.triageReason: Wave 3a fixed focus/page.tsx hydration-safe CSS vars (f-210 fix covers inline style removal).

findings.originalTitle: Score bar for one topic renders at 0% width despite non-zero score color
findings.evidence: width: '0%' with green background for a LOW-priority row.
findings.suggestion: Audit width calculation for LOW-severity topic bar.
findings.triageReason: Wave 3a addressed CSS vars but the 0% width bug on mastered items was not explicitly fixed in any wave log. Needs A_app_ui investigation in app/focus/page.tsx.

findings.originalTitle: Duplicate score '68' shown for two different topics, both labeled MED / Clinical
findings.evidence: Two consecutive rows with identical score, priority badge, and category badge.
findings.suggestion: Verify distinct topics or fix rendering loop deduplication.
findings.triageReason: Two genuinely distinct Clinical-level systems can share a score of 68; identical scores are not a bug. Audit account data creates this coincidence. No code change needed.

findings.originalTitle: Difficulty tooltip describes 'Foundations' timer as 'no timer' but page UI shows no timer confirmation
findings.evidence: Foundations tooltip says 'no timer'; no visible timer state confirmation in case workspace.
findings.suggestion: Add visible 'No Timer' label to Foundations case workspace header.
findings.triageReason: f-274 (trainer tab) covers this same Foundations timer framing question. Wave 3a added difficulty tooltips. No-timer is intentional design per DIFFICULTY_INFO constants. The framing is a pedagogical choice, not a bug.

findings.originalTitle: All case workflow steps disabled before case generation, with no affordance explaining why
findings.evidence: All six steps disabled with cursor-not-allowed and no tooltip.
findings.suggestion: Add tooltip 'Generate a case to unlock' on hover of disabled steps.
findings.triageReason: False positive: snapshot captured under focus/page.tsx but evidence describes trainer nav. Trainer nav got tooltips in commit f9fcbe0 (f-275/cc-012). Focus page has no such buttons.

findings.originalTitle: Focus page score list mixes 'Foundations' and 'Clinical' category labels with no legend
findings.evidence: Rows alternate Foundations/Clinical badges with no header explaining what they mean.
findings.suggestion: Add legend explaining difficulty badge = tier where gap was observed.
findings.triageReason: No prior wave addressed Focus page difficulty-badge legend. Minor UX improvement still actionable.

findings.originalTitle: Score thresholds for HIGH/MED/LOW priority bands are not disclosed to the learner
findings.evidence: Cutoffs (<68=HIGH, 68–83=MED, ≥84=LOW) invisible to user.
findings.suggestion: Display scoring rubric in tooltip on badge or collapsible legend.
findings.triageReason: No prior wave addressed urgency-band threshold disclosure. Minor UX improvement still actionable.

findings.originalTitle: Foundations rubric point totals do not sum to 100
findings.evidence: 24+24+36+16 = 100. Header reads '4 categories, 100 pts total'
findings.suggestion: Verify arithmetic is programmatically asserted.
findings.triageReason: Math checks out — the finding itself notes 'arithmetic is correct'. No bug exists; retained as audit verification.

findings.originalTitle: Clinical/Advanced rubric 5-category points sum to 100 but Efficiency note contradicts exclusion claim
findings.evidence: Efficiency described inline with scored dimensions, creating ambiguity about whether it's a 6th scored element.
findings.suggestion: List Efficiency as separate non-scoring indicator outside rubric table, or update header to clarify.
findings.triageReason: Reading app/help/page.tsx: the CLINICAL_ADVANCED_DIMS array has 5 items (no Efficiency row), but there is no rendered Efficiency callout visually separated from the rubric table. The rubric table implies 5 dimensions. This ambiguity was not resolved in any wave.

findings.originalTitle: Diagnosis Completeness description contradicts itself across difficulty tiers
findings.evidence: Clinical and Advanced share one rubric row with different expectations described inline.
findings.suggestion: Split into distinct rubric sections per tier.
findings.triageReason: Wave 2+3a: help page Diagnosis Completeness now explicitly states 'At Clinical, correct core earns 10–15. At Advanced, etiology/staging required for 13–15 pts.' Confirmed in code at CLINICAL_ADVANCED_DIMS line 19.

findings.originalTitle: STEMI/NSTEMI partial-credit cap uses wrong point fractions
findings.evidence: Help page STEMI FAQ: '43–44%' framing and management-critical subtype description.
findings.suggestion: Clarify STEMI vs NSTEMI is a management-critical subtype distinction, not a different pathological process.
findings.triageReason: Wave 2+Wave 3a fixed STEMI FAQ text to '43–44% (16/36 Foundations; 13/30 Clinical/Advanced)' and clarified cath-lab management distinction. FAQ now reads correctly per code.

findings.originalTitle: FAQ answer for partial credit contradicts the scoring rubric explanation
findings.evidence: FAQ says omitting 'spontaneous' is 'still marked correct'; STEMI territory omission triggers a cap. No explicit rule distinguishing management-weight qualifier from non-management qualifier.
findings.suggestion: Add explicit criteria distinguishing management-neutral vs management-weight qualifier omissions.
findings.triageReason: Wave 3a updated FAQ partial-credit answer to: 'omitting a qualifier is accepted only when it does not change management (e.g. pharyngitis vs viral pharyngitis). When qualifier carries management weight — such as STEMI territory — omitting it costs points.' Confirmed in code line 27.

findings.originalTitle: Free plan scorecard description inconsistent between Plans card and FAQ
findings.evidence: Plans card says '5 dimensions'; FAQ omits '5'. Foundations has only 4.
findings.suggestion: State 'up to 5 dimensions depending on difficulty'.
findings.triageReason: Wave 2+3a fixed Plans card to read '4 dimensions at Foundations, 5 at Clinical/Advanced'. Confirmed in code at line 150.

findings.originalTitle: Recommendation algorithm formula uses unexplained magic constant with no ceiling
findings.evidence: 1.2 multiplier unexplained; urgency range undefined for never-attempted systems.
findings.suggestion: Add note on valid range, unattempted systems treatment, and rationale for 1.2×.
findings.triageReason: Wave 1 fix and Wave 3a expanded algorithm card — confirmed in code: help page now explains '1.2× case-count factor for single-case systems reflects less reliable data point' with explicit formula shown.

findings.originalTitle: No anchor links between FAQ answers and relevant rubric sections
findings.evidence: FAQ answers duplicate rubric content without linking back to rubric sections.
findings.suggestion: Add in-page anchor links from FAQ to rubric sections.
findings.triageReason: No prior wave added anchor links to help/page.tsx. Minor UX improvement still actionable.

findings.originalTitle: Efficiency score visual position in the rubric is ambiguous for screen readers
findings.evidence: Efficiency note appears inline with scored rubric rows, no visual separator, no ARIA role.
findings.suggestion: Render as bordered callout with aria-label after rubric table.
findings.triageReason: Related to f-218. No Efficiency section visible in current help page code — needs A_app_ui fix to add distinct Efficiency callout.

findings.originalTitle: Score math contradicts displayed subscores (asthma case)
findings.evidence: History & Interview 19/24 (79%) called 'Strong targeted questioning'; 5-pt deduction for one missed item.
findings.suggestion: Reconcile rubric so single missed item doesn't cost 5/24, or enumerate all missed items to justify deduction.
findings.triageReason: SCORE↔FEEDBACK CONSISTENCY RULE (Wave 3c) requires praise language only at ≥90% of max. 79% with 'strong' praise violates this rule — either the grader is not following it or the rule needs strengthening. Needs C_grader_rules entry.

findings.originalTitle: 'Clinical' difficulty chip missing count label
findings.evidence: Filter chips: 'All (50)', 'Foundations (47)', 'Clinical', 'Advanced (3)'
findings.suggestion: Add count to Clinical chip.
findings.triageReason: Wave 2+3a fix confirmed in code: DIFF_FILTERS.map renders count = diffCounts[d] ?? 0 for all tiers including Clinical. Clinical(0) renders correctly.

findings.originalTitle: Foundations count (47) does not add up with other levels
findings.evidence: 47+3=50 only if Clinical=0; chip renders without zero label making it ambiguous.
findings.suggestion: Show 'Clinical (0)' explicitly.
findings.triageReason: Same fix as f-228 — count always shows including (0). Closed.

findings.originalTitle: Difficulty badge truncated to 'Foun' instead of full label
findings.evidence: Every visible row renders badge text 'Foun'.
findings.suggestion: Add whiteSpace:nowrap or deliberate abbreviation.
findings.triageReason: Wave 2+3a confirmed: history/page.tsx line 716 shows whiteSpace:'nowrap' and full session.difficulty text rendered.

findings.originalTitle: 'Your Diagnosis' vs 'Correct Diagnosis' column mismatch signals broken grading in STEMI rows
findings.evidence: Student: 'STEMI'; Correct: 'Inferior STEMI'; Result badge: 'Correct' — territory not required.
findings.suggestion: Grading should distinguish complete vs partial correct; badge should show 'Partial'.
findings.triageReason: Wave 10 added STEMI TERRITORIAL QUALIFIER FEEDBACK to rubric.mjs (requirement to name missing territory in feedback), but this only applies to diagnosisAccuracy feedback text, not the binary correct/incorrect flag or history-tab result badge. The badge still shows 'Correct' without territory. Needs C_grader_rules entry to require territory before setting correct=true for STEMI cases.

findings.originalTitle: STEMI graded 'Correct' without territory identification — patient safety risk
findings.evidence: Two sessions: STEMI without territory graded Correct; Inferior STEMI requires right-sided leads before nitrates.
findings.suggestion: Territory must be required for correct=true on STEMI cases.
findings.triageReason: Same as f-231 — cc-005 cross-cutting. Wave 10 STEMI TERRITORIAL QUALIFIER FEEDBACK only addresses feedback text, not the correct flag. Needs explicit C_grader_rules entry: for STEMI cases with territory in correctDiagnosis, correct=true requires territory in student diagnosis.

findings.originalTitle: ABG listed as confirming asthma diagnosis — misleading clinical teaching
findings.evidence: testOrdering feedback: 'ABG ... directly confirmed the diagnosis'
findings.suggestion: ABG informs severity, not diagnosis. Use 'informed severity stratification' language.
findings.triageReason: Wave 3c ABG-AS-CONFIRMATORY RULE explicitly forbids 'confirmed the diagnosis' language for ABG in asthma cases.

findings.originalTitle: Diagnosis Completeness score contradicts stated level expectations
findings.evidence: Feedback: 'at Foundations level this is fully complete'; Score: 11/16 (68.75%).
findings.suggestion: If 'fully complete', score should be ≥14/16; or rewrite feedback to justify 11/16.
findings.triageReason: SCORE↔FEEDBACK CONSISTENCY RULE + Wave 2 VERIFY instruction both require diagnosisCompleteness ≥ dcFoundMin when correct+Foundations. A score of 11/16 with 'fully complete' language is a clear violation of both rules but still occurring in practice. Needs C_grader_rules reinforcement.

findings.originalTitle: Notes textarea state is not persisted — learner effort lost on collapse
findings.evidence: Textarea empty with no saved value; notes lost on collapse or reload.
findings.suggestion: Persist notes to localStorage or backend on input.
findings.triageReason: Already implemented: app/history/page.tsx:454-470 auto-save via /api/sessions/notes; :102-109 + :232-237 controlled textarea with onBlur save and NotesSaveChip

findings.originalTitle: Only 3 history questions asked for a STEMI-equivalent case — unrealistically low
findings.evidence: 'Questions: 3' for asthma Foundations case with 90% score.
findings.suggestion: Clarify whether '3' refers to only clarifying questions or total; address if validating sparse history.
findings.triageReason: The 'Questions' count reflects the audit solver's behavior (3 questions per Foundations case per audit script design). Real user interaction would differ. Not an app bug; the label correctly counts chat turns.

findings.originalTitle: 'Recent trend' stat shows -1 pts with no reference period or context
findings.evidence: '-1 pts' red with no baseline period stated.
findings.suggestion: Add tooltip specifying comparison period (e.g., 'vs. prior 10 cases').
findings.triageReason: Wave 2 fixed recent-trend to show '%' instead of 'pts'. Wave 3a f-235 fix confirmed. Current code shows '−1%' label from recentTrend calculation.

findings.originalTitle: Avg Score (88) and Correct Rate (94%) are statistically incompatible
findings.evidence: Avg Score 88, Correct Rate 94% — incompatible if wrong cases score 0.
findings.suggestion: Label both metrics clearly to explain they measure different things (rubric-based vs binary).
findings.triageReason: No prior wave addressed this label distinction on progress page. Avg Score is rubric-based partial credit; Correct Rate is binary. Both showing same page needs labeling clarity. Needs A_app_ui fix.

findings.originalTitle: Performance table row counts sum to 65 but Cardiovascular has 10 while all others have 5
findings.evidence: Finding itself retracts: 10 + 11×5 = 65. Correct.
findings.suggestion: No action needed — retained as verification.
findings.triageReason: Finding itself states 'No action needed — this finding is retracted.' Confirmed false positive.

findings.originalTitle: Cardiovascular 'Clinical' column shows '—' but 'Advanced' column shows a score
findings.evidence: Cardiovascular Advanced = 88; Clinical = '—'. Other 11 systems all-Foundations.
findings.suggestion: Verify difficulty-tier bucketing; Clinical should precede Advanced.
findings.triageReason: The audit account completed one Advanced Cardiovascular case from the trainer page without doing a Clinical case first. The app doesn't enforce sequential progression. This is data, not a bug.

findings.originalTitle: Page heading says 'over time' twice — redundant subtitle duplicates the heading
findings.evidence: h1: 'Progress over time'; subtitle: 'Your learning trajectory over time'
findings.suggestion: Change subtitle to additive content like case count and system breakdown.
findings.triageReason: Already implemented: app/progress/page.tsx:88 subtitle is now "${totalCases} case(s) tracked" — no duplicate "over time" phrase

findings.originalTitle: No low-performing system is surfaced — all Avg scores are 83–89, hindering self-directed study
findings.evidence: All 12 system rows show green scores 83–89.
findings.suggestion: Flag scores below configurable threshold with warning color or cross-reference Focus Areas.
findings.triageReason: The audit account genuinely has high scores across all systems. The Focus Areas page already handles relative weakness ranking. The progress table shows absolute scores; adding a relative weakness flag would duplicate Focus Areas logic.

findings.originalTitle: Sort controls on Performance Breakdown table are non-functional
findings.evidence: interactionLog: [] — no click handlers exercised.
findings.suggestion: Confirm click event listeners attached to .sortable headers.
findings.triageReason: Wave 3 confirmed false positive — sort indicator already wired; auditor missed dynamic render. clickable rows → /history?system=... confirmed in Wave 3a PerformanceBreakdown.tsx fix.

findings.originalTitle: 'Clinical' and 'Advanced' columns are nearly always '—', providing no differential learning signal
findings.evidence: 11/12 systems show '—' for both Clinical and Advanced columns.
findings.suggestion: Hide empty columns or add 'Try a Clinical case →' prompt.
findings.triageReason: No prior wave addressed empty-column UX for progress table. Still actionable minor improvement.

findings.originalTitle: Avg Time (7m 44s) has no benchmark or trend context
findings.evidence: 'Avg Time: 7m 44s' with no target range or peer context.
findings.suggestion: Add target range or median benchmark label.
findings.triageReason: Wave 3a f-246 fix: Avg Time now shows median benchmark. Confirmed in Wave 3a fix log.

findings.originalTitle: '3-case avg' rolling window is too small to smooth noise for 65 cases
findings.evidence: 3-case rolling average on 65 data points is highly volatile.
findings.suggestion: Offer toggle for window size (3/5/10 cases).
findings.triageReason: Wave 3a ScoreOverTime.tsx: 30d/90d/All time-range selector added. The window-size concern is addressed by the time filter.

findings.originalTitle: No date range or time axis labels visible on 'Score Over Time' chart
findings.evidence: Canvas element with no visible axis label text or date range filter.
findings.suggestion: Ensure x-axis tick labels accessible; add date-range selector.
findings.triageReason: Wave 3a ScoreOverTime.tsx: 30d/90d/All time-range selector and aria-label on canvas added.

findings.originalTitle: Overall accuracy stat contradicts system-level data
findings.suggestion: Recalculate from same data source; ensure sync.
findings.triageReason: Wave 3a f-253 fix: 1-decimal accuracy in review page. The 92% vs 91.67% rounds to 92% with standard rounding — consistent.

findings.originalTitle: Avg score stat conflicts with per-case score data
findings.evidence: Avg score 90/100; dimension averages also ~90%; both labeled the same, different calculations.
findings.suggestion: Label headline as case-level average; dimension section separately.
findings.triageReason: No prior wave addressed the labeling distinction between case-level avg score and dimension-breakdown avg. Both appear as '90' — needs label differentiation in review page.

findings.originalTitle: Score Distribution histogram zero-count bars render with non-zero height
findings.evidence: 0–19, 20–39, 40–59 bars render with min-h-[2px] height despite zero cases.
findings.suggestion: Set bar height to 0 or hide when count is 0.
findings.triageReason: Already implemented: app/review/page.tsx:467 uses bucket.count === 0 ? 0 : Math.max(2, pct*100) — zero bars already hidden

findings.originalTitle: 7-case rolling average line starts at case 1, not case 7
findings.evidence: Rolling-average polyline begins at very first point.
findings.suggestion: Start rendering rolling average only from 7th data point, or re-label as 'expanding window avg'.
findings.triageReason: Wave 3a f-251/f-253 review fixes — the rolling average behavior is a known accepted pattern (expanding window for first N-1 points). Already labeled appropriately.

findings.originalTitle: SVG chart data points have no keyboard or focus interaction
findings.evidence: SVG circles have no tabindex, role, or aria attributes.
findings.suggestion: Add tabindex='0', role='img', aria-label to each circle.
findings.triageReason: No prior wave addressed accessibility (a11y) of SVG chart circles. Actionable a11y bug.

findings.originalTitle: System × Difficulty table shows only one difficulty tier, masking curriculum gaps
findings.evidence: All 12 systems show '—' for Clinical and Advanced.
findings.suggestion: Add CTA within empty columns: 'Try a Clinical case →'.
findings.triageReason: No prior wave addressed empty-column CTA in review page. Same pattern as f-245 (progress page). Actionable minor UX improvement.

findings.originalTitle: Weakest area (Hematologic/Oncologic 0%) has no actionable study recommendation
findings.evidence: Hematologic 0% system row; no 'Study this topic' link or next-case suggestion.
findings.suggestion: Add 'Focus area' widget linking to new case in lowest-performing system.
findings.triageReason: No prior wave addressed surfacing a next-case link from the review page. The Focus page handles this but review page has no cross-link. Actionable UX improvement.

findings.originalTitle: All 12 cases share the same date (5/16/2026), limiting trend utility
findings.evidence: Every score-trend circle tooltip ends with '· 5/16/2026'.
findings.suggestion: Label x-axis as 'Case #' (ordinal) instead of implying time.
findings.triageReason: Audit account ran all 12 cases in one day. Real user data would have date spread. Not an app bug; timestamps correctly reflect actual session times.

findings.originalTitle: 'Systems tried: 12/12' denominator is misleading at Foundations-only coverage
findings.evidence: 'Systems tried: 12/12' despite only Foundations tier attempted.
findings.suggestion: Change to '12/36 combinations' or tooltip clarifying metric counts distinct systems.
findings.triageReason: Already implemented: app/review/page.tsx:358-362 renders "${n} / 12 (Foundations only)" when only Foundations tier attempted

findings.originalTitle: Dark mode toggle button not found / non-functional
findings.evidence: toggle-dark-mode action: 'button not found'
findings.suggestion: Verify chip buttons are wired to state and have correct accessible labels.
findings.triageReason: Wave 3 confirmed false positive — theme chip buttons correctly enable on populated state. Audit automation tested with empty inputs.

findings.originalTitle: Theme chip buttons lack persist logic — selection resets on reload
findings.evidence: No visible Save or onChange handler for chip group.
findings.suggestion: Wire chip clicks to write to localStorage.
findings.triageReason: No prior wave explicitly verified theme chip persistence. The settings page reads medtrainer_color_scheme from localStorage on load but the onChange wiring for chip clicks is not confirmed. Needs A_app_ui verification.

findings.originalTitle: Weekly case goal input accepts values beyond stated max with no validation message
findings.evidence: HTML min/max advisory only; no server-side inline error shown.
findings.suggestion: Add client-side clamp and inline error on out-of-range save.
findings.triageReason: Wave 3a settings fix: onChange handler already uses Math.max(1, Math.min(effectiveCap, ...)) — confirmed in code at line 306. Value is clamped on input.

findings.originalTitle: Free plan case limit contradicts itself: '2 cases per day' vs weekly goal up to 14
findings.evidence: Free plan: 2/day cap; input shows goal up to 14 without explaining the daily cap limits weekly total.
findings.suggestion: Display contextual note that Free users are limited to 2 cases/day so effective weekly ceiling is 14.
findings.triageReason: Wave 3a settings fix: effectiveCap displayed dynamically from rest days. Help text now reads 'Free plan: up to 2 cases per active day (max X/week with your current rest days).' Confirmed in code at line 311.

findings.originalTitle: Rest days selection has no Save button of its own
findings.evidence: Single 'Save preferences' button saves all training prefs together without communicating this.
findings.suggestion: Add label 'Save all training preferences' to clarify scope.
findings.triageReason: No prior wave addressed the button label ambiguity. Minor UX improvement still actionable.

findings.originalTitle: Upgrade to Pro uses mailto: link — fails silently when no email client is configured
findings.evidence: Upgrade to Pro →
findings.suggestion: Replace with in-app contact form or web-based checkout link.
findings.triageReason: No prior wave addressed the mailto: upgrade path. Confirmed still present. Actionable UX fix.

findings.originalTitle: Notification preferences saved but explicitly non-functional — misleading to learners
findings.evidence: Checked checkboxes for 'Daily case reminders' with note 'Email sending is coming soon'.
findings.suggestion: Disable checkboxes with 'Coming soon' badge or remove section until feature ships.
findings.triageReason: Wave 3a settings fix: help text clearly states 'Email sending is coming soon — your preferences are saved and will take effect when enabled.' Confirmed in code at line 354.

findings.originalTitle: Password fields ship with autocomplete not explicitly set
findings.evidence: Three password inputs with no autocomplete attribute.
findings.suggestion: Add autocomplete='current-password' / autocomplete='new-password'.
findings.triageReason: No autocomplete attributes found in settings/page.tsx. No prior wave addressed this. Minor a11y/UX fix still needed.

findings.originalTitle: Theme chips use Unicode symbols (☀ ☾ ⬤) without accessible labels
findings.evidence: No aria-label on theme chip buttons; screen readers read raw Unicode character names.
findings.suggestion: Add aria-label='Light theme' etc. or wrap symbol in aria-hidden span.
findings.triageReason: No aria-label attributes found in settings/page.tsx. No prior wave addressed this a11y gap.

findings.originalTitle: Display name field has no Save feedback — success/failure state is invisible to user
findings.evidence: 'Save profile' button with no visible success toast or button state change.
findings.suggestion: Add transient success indicator after save.
findings.triageReason: Already implemented: app/settings/page.tsx:43,87-97,220-226 sets profileStatus=saving|saved|error and renders statusText next to Save profile button

findings.originalTitle: Duplicate 'Generate Case' buttons with no behavioral differentiation
findings.evidence: Header button + main area 'Generate Your First Case' button both present.
findings.suggestion: Retain only one canonical entry point.
findings.triageReason: Wave 3a f-276: removed duplicate generate button. Trainer now uses header button as primary with main-area empty-state button only shown when no case active.

findings.originalTitle: Header 'Generate Case' button lacks disabled state before case is active
findings.evidence: Header button has no disabled attribute; can be clicked mid-session.
findings.suggestion: Disable header Generate Case button while case in progress.
findings.triageReason: Code at line 1725: header button is disabled={generating} only (while generating). Once case is active and generating=false, button is re-enabled allowing accidental case replacement. No prior wave addressed mid-session disable. Needs A_app_ui fix.

findings.originalTitle: Clinical level tooltip description inconsistent: 'Clinical' timer 22 min but 'Advanced' 15 min
findings.evidence: Advanced (harder) has shorter timer (15 min) than Clinical (22 min).
findings.suggestion: Add explicit instructional copy explaining shorter Advanced timer is intentional time-pressure mechanic.
findings.triageReason: Wave 3a f-279: difficulty tooltips with timers added. The shorter Advanced timer is intentional design (simulates rounds time-pressure per help page description 'simulating rounds time-pressure'). Confirmed as intentional.

findings.originalTitle: Foundations difficulty described as 'no timer' — inappropriate framing for clinical reasoning training
findings.evidence: 'Foundations — no timer. Output: diagnosis only.'
findings.suggestion: Add optional soft timer or post-case elapsed time display.
findings.triageReason: No-timer at Foundations is intentional pedagogical design decision. Adding time-awareness at Foundations level is a product scope change, not a bug fix.

findings.originalTitle: All nav steps disabled with no visual progress indicator or affordance
findings.evidence: All six nav buttons disabled with cursor-not-allowed and no unlock tooltip.
findings.suggestion: Add tooltip 'Generate a case to unlock' on hover of disabled steps; add step numbers.
findings.triageReason: Wave 3a added step numbers in nav (f-280 fix) but code shows no title/tooltip attribute on disabled nav buttons (confirmed at line 1852–1866). No tooltip text shown for disabled state. Needs A_app_ui fix to add title attribute on disabled nav buttons.

findings.originalTitle: SOAP template button in Case Notes panel is non-functional with no feedback
findings.evidence: 'SOAP template' button with no wired action.
findings.suggestion: Populate textarea with SOAP scaffold or disable with tooltip.
findings.triageReason: Wave 3 f-281 confirmed false positive — SOAP template button is already wired. Confirmed in code: onClick sets notes.mode='soap' and populates SOAP_TEMPLATE content at line 2075.

findings.originalTitle: 'Case Notes' label uses 'caution' color with no explanatory context
findings.evidence: Case Notes
findings.suggestion: Use neutral label color or add tooltip explaining caution styling.
findings.triageReason: Wave 10 claimed to fix f-285 (Case Notes color) but code at line 2060 still shows text-caution on 'Case Notes' label. Fix did not land or was reverted. Needs A_app_ui fix.

findings.originalTitle: Patient Interview panel has no placeholder case/patient context while idle
findings.evidence: Empty panel with only 'Generate a case to start interviewing the patient.'
findings.suggestion: Display sample interaction or animated preview of interview experience.
findings.triageReason: Animated interview preview is a non-trivial UX feature addition beyond bug-fix scope. The existing placeholder text is functional. Deferred as product enhancement.

findings.originalTitle: Dictation button in Case Notes has no disabled state when no case is active
findings.evidence: Dictation button enabled with no active case.
findings.suggestion: Disable dictation button until case generated.
findings.triageReason: Wave 3a f-282 fix: disabled textarea/mic when no case. Confirmed — MicButton receives disabled prop based on caseData state.

findings.originalTitle: System filter dropdown includes 'Toxicologic' and 'Trauma' but these map poorly to organ-system categories
findings.evidence: 'Toxicologic' and 'Trauma' as options alongside 'Cardiovascular', 'Respiratory'.
findings.suggestion: Separate into <optgroup> 'By System' vs 'By Context'.
findings.triageReason: The taxonomy mixing is a known product design choice — Toxicologic and Trauma are established case categories used throughout the app. Restructuring the dropdown requires schema-level category changes. Out of scope.

findings.originalTitle: History score awarded inconsistently relative to missed key questions
findings.suggestion: Implement proportional key-question coverage floor: each missed designated key question deducts defined fixed proportion of History dimension score.
findings.triageReason: KEY-QUESTION ELICITATION RULE exists but provides no proportional deduction floor. SCORE↔FEEDBACK CONSISTENCY RULE addresses praise/score mismatch but not the floor itself. This cross-cutting pattern (f-035, f-039, f-063) requires a new C_grader_rules entry imposing a per-missed-key-question deduction.

findings.originalTitle: Grading penalizes students for citing vitals that ARE present in caseData
findings.evidence: Both cases accuse student of fabricating vitals that appear verbatim in caseData.vitals.
findings.suggestion: Grader must cross-reference student-cited values against caseData.vitals before issuing fabrication warning.
findings.triageReason: GRADING TEXT FIDELITY RULE prevents inventing tests but does not address vitals cross-referencing before fabrication warnings. Covers same cases as f-047 and f-048. Needs C_grader_rules entry.

findings.originalTitle: Test ordering scores near-full despite clinically significant missing tests
findings.evidence: Pivotal tests missing (thoracentesis, MRI brain) yet testOrdering scored near-max with 'complete' feedback.
findings.suggestion: Define 'pivotal tests' per case; any pivotal test not ordered triggers mandatory deduction with explicit named feedback.
findings.triageReason: No existing rule creates a pivotal-test mandatory deduction concept. Wave 7 testOrdering band recalibration addressed band logic but not management-altering pivotal tests. Needs C_grader_rules entry.

findings.originalTitle: Scoring rubric dimension weights applied inconsistently across tabs and cases
findings.evidence: 'Fully complete' answer gets 68.75%; 'Strong' history with 21% deduction; STEMI without territory graded Correct.
findings.suggestion: Centralize scoring logic; auto-generate qualitative feedback from numeric deduction calculation.
findings.triageReason: Multiple wave rules address individual symptoms of this problem (SCORE↔FEEDBACK CONSISTENCY, VERIFY floor, WRONG DIAGNOSIS cap) but the systemic inconsistency persists. Needs a comprehensive C_grader_rules reinforcement entry tying all rules together.

findings.originalTitle: STEMI graded correct without territory identification — patient safety risk
findings.evidence: Two history sessions: STEMI without territory → Result: Correct. Inferior STEMI requires right-sided leads before nitrates.
findings.suggestion: Territory required for correct=true on all STEMI cases.
findings.triageReason: Wave 10 STEMI TERRITORIAL QUALIFIER FEEDBACK requires feedback text to name territory but does NOT require territory for correct=true. Needs explicit C_grader_rules entry: when correctDiagnosis contains territory qualifier, correct=true requires student diagnosis to include territory.

findings.originalTitle: Wrong or unsupported diagnoses receive passing total scores due to high workup sub-scores
findings.evidence: case-8: wrong dx → score 72 from high testOrdering. case-9: unconfirmed co-dx near-full credit. case-15: wrong dx, testOrdering 'complete'.
findings.suggestion: Implement diagnosis-accuracy gate: wrong primary diagnosis caps total score at defined maximum (e.g., 65/100).
findings.triageReason: Wave 2 WRONG DIAGNOSIS cap applies to diagnosisCompleteness dimension only. No total-score cap for wrong primary diagnosis exists. This is the most significant remaining grader gap. Needs C_grader_rules entry.

findings.originalTitle: HPI states patient denies a symptom that hidden history confirms is present
findings.evidence: Multiple cases: HPI outright denies symptoms present in hiddenHistory.
findings.suggestion: HPI may only deny symptoms not contradicted by hiddenHistory; use qualified language otherwise.
findings.triageReason: Wave 3b HPI-HIDDEN HISTORY NON-CONTRADICTION RULE explicitly forbids outright denial in HPI when hiddenHistory contradicts. Addresses all four referenced cases.

findings.originalTitle: Unordered test results surfaced to student — simulation fidelity broken
findings.evidence: Pleural fluid results shown without thoracentesis order; PBS accessible without explicit order; passive SI revealed without direct question.
findings.suggestion: Enforce strict test-gating at data layer: no result rendered unless corresponding test in testsOrdered.
findings.triageReason: Wave 5 ORDERED-TESTS SCORING CONTRACT addresses grader side. Platform-level gating exists (trainer/page.tsx:3389) but procedural results (thoracentesis, PBS) may bypass it if categorized under wrong array. Needs A_app_ui verification and B_case_gen_rules fix for categorization.

findings.originalTitle: Non-laboratory procedures listed under availableImaging category
findings.evidence: PBS, ECG, Thoracentesis in availableImaging.
findings.suggestion: Introduce 'Procedures' category alongside availableLabs and availableImaging.
findings.triageReason: No existing rule creates a Procedures category. Needs B_case_gen_rules entry banning PBS/ECG/Thoracentesis from availableImaging.

findings.originalTitle: Clinically significant hidden findings never discoverable or graded
findings.evidence: Tophus, DRE findings, working-diagnosis evolution: all in hidden fields with no key-question trigger and no grading weight.
findings.suggestion: All hiddenHistory/hiddenSymptom findings that change diagnosis or management must have key-question trigger and grading weight.
findings.triageReason: Wave 3b KEY-QUESTION HIDDEN-ANSWER COVERAGE requires hidden answers to align with keyQuestions but doesn't mandate classification-changing findings be assigned triggers. Needs B_case_gen_rules entry.

findings.originalTitle: Unexplained or mislabeled lab results with no teaching point resolution
findings.evidence: MCV 101 unexplained in CLL/AIHA; HbF 6% flagged high in SCD; TG 310 no threshold context.
findings.suggestion: Every non-normal status flag must have corresponding teaching point explaining clinical significance.
findings.triageReason: No existing rule covers this requirement for ALL abnormal lab values. Partial coverage exists (Wave 3b HbSS MCV, MORNING-DARK-URINE) but no universal rule. Needs B_case_gen_rules entry.

findings.originalTitle: All six case workflow nav steps disabled with no affordance explaining prerequisite
findings.evidence: Identical disabled-step pattern in both focus and trainer tabs; no tooltip in either.
findings.suggestion: Add consistent tooltip on all disabled nav steps from shared component.
findings.triageReason: Code inspection confirms no title/tooltip on disabled nav buttons in trainer/page.tsx (line 1852). Focus page has no nav step component. Needs A_app_ui fix in both pages.

findings.originalTitle: Teaching points describe management actions without anchoring to the specific patient
findings.evidence: Teaching points present generic management options without identifying which applies to the actual patient.
findings.suggestion: Teaching points must anchor at least one management recommendation to a specific patient finding.
findings.triageReason: Wave 3b MANAGEMENT TEACHING POINT RULE requires a concrete management pearl, but no rule requires patient-anchored specificity. Same pattern as f-049. Needs B_case_gen_rules entry.
